recent_lines: Checking the weather forecast won't change what it's doing outside.
My "just spin the legs" ride has a 47-minute Strava segment in it.
Every cyclist I pass on a climb is suddenly racing me.
My Garmin has seen things I will never tell my coach.
Why does my "easy gear" still require an apology to my lungs?
Three hours in. Still mentally rewriting my training plan instead of following it.
Nobody told my legs the first kilometre was supposed to be easy.
The café stop was supposed to be five minutes. That was forty minutes ago.
My average speed drops the second another rider pulls alongside.
I own four bikes. I ride one. The others are "in rotation.
My "recovery week" has more climbing than my last race.
The group ride "no-drop" policy quietly ended at kilometre 14.
Knees screaming. Heart rate spiked. Cadence collapsed. Garmin says "moderate effort.
Checked the weather four times. Still left in the wrong kit.
The climb looked shorter on Komoot.
